research.interface: Snowflake SQL API v2, POST /api/v2/statements + GET /api/v2/statements/{statementHandle}?partition=N (GA; jsonv2 result format)

official_sources.title: Snowflake SQL API reference
official_sources.used_for: POST /api/v2/statements and GET /api/v2/statements/{statementHandle}; the partition query parameter; ResultSet schema with resultSetMetaData (numRows, format, rowType with name/type/length/precision/scale/nullable, partitionInfo with rowCount/uncompressedSize/compressedSize); QueryFailureStatus schema; status codes 200/404/422/429

official_sources.title: Handling responses
official_sources.used_for: results split into partitions sized by the server; the first response carries partition 0 inline in data; additional partitions retrieved with GET ?partition=N; rows encoded as arrays of strings with JSON null for SQL NULL; partition responses carry data only, metadata is not repeated

official_sources.title: Submitting a request to execute SQL statements
official_sources.used_for: required Authorization/Accept/Content-Type/User-Agent headers, requestId UUID query parameter, request body fields statement/timeout/database/schema/warehouse/role and their case-sensitivity

research.language_rationale: Snowflake's Go driver targets database/sql for interactive use; for a batch exporter that must see partition boundaries, raw sizes and string-exact values, the documented SQL API v2 REST surface is the intended driverless integration path, and Go's net/http + encoding/json stdlib is the idiomatic zero-dependency client.

verified_facts: Query results from POST /api/v2/statements are returned in server-chosen partitions: resultSetMetaData.partitionInfo lists one entry per partition with rowCount and uncompressedSize (compressedSize on later partitions), and the data array in the 200 response body is partition 0.

Additional partitions are fetched with GET /api/v2/statements/{statementHandle}?partition=N (N is the 0-based index into partitionInfo); the partition response carries the row data and does not repeat the result-set metadata.

rowType describes each column with name, type, length, precision, scale and nullable; row values are transferred as strings regardless of SQL type, and SQL NULL arrives as JSON null, so a faithful reader must preserve the exact string encoding and track null-ness separately.

Every request carries Authorization: Bearer {token}, Accept: application/json and a real User-Agent; X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type declares KEYPAIR_JWT/OAUTH/PROGRAMMATIC_ACCESS_TOKEN, and POST bodies add Content-Type: application/json plus a requestId UUID query parameter.
A 200 ResultSet reports code 090001 with statementHandle and statementStatusUrl; execution errors answer 422 with a QueryFailureStatus carrying code, sqlState, message and statementHandle.

Statement context fields (database, schema, warehouse, role) are case-sensitive and optional; a 404 from the statements endpoints means the resource (statement handle) is unknown — results are not retained indefinitely, so an expired handle must stop the read rather than be retried.
